name: NYU Precollege — Writing Workshop
session_dates: Session A: June 20 – July 3; Session B: July 11 – July 24; Session C: July 25 – August 7, 2026

cost.notes: Per session; NYU Precollege also has a general program fee

essays.prompt: Writing sample required. Essay: Why do you want to study writing at NYU?
tips: Show how your interests connect to NYC as a literary city, not just NYU as a name

outcomes.college_impact: Moderate signal at NYU specifically. NYU Precollege is more of an academic preview than a prestigious summer program.
outcomes.typical_results: Students get NYU course credit and a transcript record; useful for demonstrating college-level performance.

what_they_look_for: Clear interest in writing or journalism
Maturity to handle NYC campus life

blurbs.short: A 2-week pre-college writing workshop at NYU for rising juniors and seniors to experience college-level writing and earn college credit.
blurbs.long: NYU Precollege Writing offers a college-level writing course to high school students over the summer. Unlike more specialized programs like Iowa or Kenyon, NYU Precollege Writing is primarily a preview of college academics — it gives students a taste of what a college writing class is like and results in an official NYU transcript with transferable credit. The program is less selective than the elite writing workshops (IYWS, Kenyon, TASP) and is more accessible. For students interested specifically in NYU's creative writing, journalism, or English programs, it provides useful exposure and a demonstrated interest in the school. For students looking for the most prestigious summer writing experience, Iowa or Kenyon are stronger choices.

curriculum: College-level writing workshop, readings, assignments, faculty feedback, NYU campus life.
